title: thymus praecox subsp. ligusticus
intro: Thymus praecox subsp. ligusticus, also known as Italian thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus praecox plant, which is a low-growing, evergreen herb native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective against various infections and diseases. The health benefits of Italian thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Italian thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb, and can be used in cooking, as well as in traditional medicine.
